data.title: Shafer Vineyards' One Point Five Cabernet Sauvignon now in United Airlines Polaris business class.
data.description: In the heart of Napa Valley, Shafer Vineyards, a prestigious winery with a rich history, revealed on July 24, 2025, that their iconic Cabernet Sauvignon, One Point Five, is now featured on United Airlines flights within the United Polaris business class. This collaboration brings the esteemed wine to passengers, enhancing their in-flight experience.

data.title: Acushnet Holdings Corp. will announce Q2 2025 financial results on August 7, 2025, at 6:45 a.m. Eastern Time.
data.description: Acushnet Holdings Corp. (NYSE: GOLF) based in Fairhaven, Mass., is set to release its second quarter 2025 financial results on August 7, 2025, around 6:45 a.m. Eastern Time. The company will make the results accessible through its Investor Relations website (http://www.acushnetholdingscorp.com/ir) as well as on the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's website. Investors and stakeholders can expect insights into Acushnet's performance during this period from the upcoming announcement.

data.title: Advance Auto Parts shared preliminary Q2 2025 financial highlights; focused on strategic goals.
data.description: In Raleigh, North Carolina, Advance Auto Parts, Inc. (NYSE: AAP), a prominent automotive aftermarket parts supplier in North America catering to professional installers and DIY customers, released early financial highlights for the second quarter of 2025. The company is concentrating on executing strategic plans to enhance business operations. The preliminary results for the second quarter show promising figures in line with the higher end of expectations.

data.title: Advance Auto Parts to offer $1,500 million in senior unsecured notes with two tranches due in 2030 and 2033.
data.description: Advance Auto Parts, Inc., a prominent automotive aftermarket parts supplier in North America catering to both professionals and DIY customers, has unveiled a senior unsecured notes offering totaling $1.5 billion. The offering comprises two tranches of notes maturing in 2030 and 2033. This private transaction, exempt from registration requirements, is geared towards furthering the company's financial endeavors.

data.title: Wallbox teams with Leap to launch VPPs in California and New York. Wallbox Rewards offers incentives for grid flexibility.
data.description: In Barcelona, Spain, Wallbox (NYSE: WBX), a worldwide electric vehicle (EV) charging and energy management solutions provider, recently introduced its virtual power plants (VPPs) in California and New York. This new development comes as a result of a partnership with Leap, a prominent VPP platform. These VPPs are included in Wallbox Rewards, a fresh smart charging program, allowing users to earn incentives by supporting grid flexibility through their contribution.

data.title: Veracode introduces advancements in Package Firewall and Risk Manager to enhance software security in the cloud era.
data.description: Veracode, headquartered in Burlington, Massachusetts, has recently introduced upgrades to Package Firewall and Risk Manager, designed to offer assurance, context, and continuity throughout the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC). These enhancements aim to improve security measures for organizations, with a focus on safeguarding software integrity. The company's advancements in Package Firewall and Risk Manager signify a commitment to enhancing their services and products for clients.

data.title: S&T Bancorp, Inc. reports Q2 2025 net income of $31.9M, 1.32% ROA, and 8.91% ROE. Total assets $9.8B. Nonperforming assets $21.3M.
data.description: S&T Bancorp, Inc. reported a net income of $31.9 million for Q2 2025, compared to $33.4 million in Q1 2025 and $34.4 million in Q2 2024. Key figures include return on average assets of 1.32%, return on average equity of 8.91%, and net interest income growth of $3.3 million. Their total assets were $9.8 billion, with total deposits increasing by $28.0 million. The provision for credit losses was $2.0 million in Q2 2025, and nonperforming assets decreased to $21.3 million. S&T Bancorp maintains strong capital ratios, and key financial data is detailed in the article for investors to consider. This information reveals the bank's performance, growth, and stability in the market.

data.title: Cryptocurrency market down, altcoins lagging behind Bitcoin. Prices: Bitcoin $118,742.21, Ethereum $3,648.82, XRP $3.13, Dogecoin $0.2374. Trader data: 310,509 traders liquidated, $956.46 million lost in 24 hours. Trader Notes: Bitcoin in week three of uptrend phase.
data.description: The cryptocurrency market experienced a decline on Thursday, with altcoins performing worse than Bitcoin following a period of strength. Prices for various cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin at $118,742.21, Ethereum at $3,648.82, Solana at $185.82, XRP at $3.13, Dogecoin at $0.2374, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001367. Significant data reveals a 13.4% decrease in large transactions for Bitcoin and a 5.2% increase for Ethereum. Daily active addresses saw a 6.2% growth for Bitcoin but dropped by 4.8% for Ethereum. The past 24 hours saw 310,509 traders liquidated, resulting in a total of $956.46 million losses. Notably, there were net outflows of $85.96 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs, while spot Ethereum ETFs witnessed net inflows of $332.2 million according to SoSoValue data. Rekt Capital analysis indicates that Bitcoin is currently in week three of its price discovery uptrend two, a phase historically characterized by continued upward momentum. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
